title: lappula himalayensis
intro: Lappula himalayensis, also known as Himalayan lappula, is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. This plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Lappula himalayensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
